title: Represent multiplication on the number line
description: Practice representing multiplication on a number line.
topic_path: Represent multiplication on the number line

hints:
$\maroonD{4} \times \blueD{7} = \underbrace{\blueD{7} + \blueD{7} + \blueD{7} + \blueD{7}}_{\maroonD{4}\blueD{\text{ sevens}}}$
When we add a number, we jump that distance to the right on the number line.

To show $\blueD{7} + \blueD{7} + \blueD{7} + \blueD{7}$, we need to show $\maroonD{4}$ jumps of $\blueD{7}$ units.

[[☃ image 1]]

This number line best represents $4\times7$:

[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which two expressions does the number line represent?**

![A number line labeled 0, 8, 16, 24, 32, 40, 48. Arrows point right from each number to the next.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/013e91799ff63fabb892e41eb2800d30f1ae5fb2)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: When we jump right on the number line, we add the distance we've moved.

![The same number line as above, with each arrow labeled, plus 8](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ac6f93b18c39d1fb8f63591215ca6c7a57878a85)

The number line represents this addition expression:

$\blueE{8+8+8+8+8+8}$

![The same number line with the full span of the arrows labeled, 6 equal jumps](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ea4a16c204f8e42459e459222986224b7321f669)

Because every jump is the same size, we can also write it as a multiplication expression.

$\maroonE{6}$ jumps of $\blueE{8}$ units $=\maroonE{6}\times\blueE{8}$ units

The number line represents these expressions:

- $8+8+8+8+8+8$
- $6 \times 8$.

choices.content: $6 \times 8$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which two expressions does the number line represent?**

![A number line labeled 0, 7, 14, 21. Arrows point right from each number to the next.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/83238135fbf16a90bfb41116dcebd5d13ccbd628)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: When we jump right on the number line, we add the distance we've moved.

![The same number line as above, with each arrow labeled, plus 7](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c1ff9e282aa1aac0947c63c96fa383f41aaa6dcc)

The number line represents this addition expression:

$\blueE{7+7+7}$

![The same number line with the full span of the arrows labeled, 3 equal jumps](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c5479b97c88238bce244934cabca16a9a1644f1b)

Because every jump is the same size, we can also write it as a multiplication expression.

$\maroonE{3}$ jumps of $\blueE{7}$ units $=\maroonE{3}\times\blueE{7}$ units

The number line represents these expressions:

- $7+7+7$
- $3 \times 7$

choices.content: $3 \times 7$
$7 \times 7 \times 7$

choices.content: $10 \times 2$
$5 \times 2$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the number line to evaluate $9\times 5$.**

[[☃ label-image 1]]

$9 \times 5 =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: $\maroonD{9} \times \blueD{5} = \underbrace{\blueD{5} + \blueD{5} + \blueD{5} + \blueD{5} + \blueD{5} + \blueD{5} + \blueD{5} + \blueD{5} + \blueD{5}}_{\maroonD9\blueD{\text{ fives}}}$

When we add a number, we jump that distance to the right on the number line.

To show $\blueD{5} + \blueD{5} + \blueD{5} + \blueD{5} + \blueD{5} + \blueD{5} + \blueD{5} + \blueD{5} + \blueD{5}$, we need to show $\maroonD{9}$ jumps of $\blueD{5}$ units.

![The same number line as above, with each arrow labeled, plus 5](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/41b6249a8c169358946e888698e1543b6db098f5)

$15+5=\purpleC{20}$

![The same number line with the first blank labeled 20](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6b3c9a4e7ac918eb2839873a1ef9007a60544802)

$40+5=\purpleC{45}$

![The same number line with the second blank labeled 45](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f9db1a770082515682709ce20934597a24dc7f1f)

The last jump leaves us at $45$.

$\begin{align}
\maroonD{9} \times \blueD{5} &= \blueD{5}+\blueD{5}+\blueD{5}+\blueD{5}+\blueD{5}+\blueD{5}+\blueD{5}+\blueD{5}+\blueD{5}\\\\
&=45
\end{align}$

The completed number line:

![The completed number line with the first blank labeled 20 and the second blank labeled 45](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/78bef08c55fc5a7fac6f512fc845d09f478a97c9)

$9\times 5=45$

options.imageAlt: A number line labeled 0, 5, 10, 15, blank, 25, 30, 35, 40, blank. Arrows point right from each number to the next.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the number line to evaluate $3\times 8$.**

[[☃ label-image 1]]

$3 \times 8 =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: $\maroonD{3} \times \blueD{8} = \underbrace{\blueD{8}+\blueD{8}+\blueD{8}}_{\maroonD{3}\blueD{\text{ eights}}}$

When we add a number, we jump that distance to the right on the number line.

To show $\blueD{8}+\blueD{8}+\blueD{8}$, we need to show $\maroonD{3}$ jumps of $\blueD{8}$ units.

![The same number line as above, with each arrow labeled, plus 8](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e08207e601a7d6eb5ed2ca0bc8e5a8ebcefcd456)

$0+8=\purpleC{8}$

![The same number line with the first blank labeled 8](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1f99d0953d23f432e5546e70b2f9519ac3f3d108)

$16+8=\purpleC{24}$

![The same number line with the second blank labeled 24](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/38382099bab5c0f4089b2fa2f2c926033f1ea451)

The last jump leaves us at $24$.

$\begin{align}
\maroonD{3} \times \blueD{8} &= \blueD{8}+\blueD{8}+\blueD{8}\\\\
&=24
\end{align}$

The completed number line:

![The completed number line with the first blank labeled 8 and the second blank labeled 24](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a7ab7920c37f8e7c04f73416260a842673b62509)

$3\times 8=24$

options.imageAlt: A number line labeled 0, blank, 16, blank. Arrows point right from each number to the next.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the number line to evaluate $4\times 6$.**

[[☃ label-image 1]]

$4 \times 6 =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: $\maroonD{4} \times \blueD{6} = \underbrace{\blueD{6}+\blueD{6}+\blueD{6}+\blueD{6}}_{\maroonD{4}\blueD{\text{ sixes}}}$

When we add a number, we jump that distance to the right on the number line.

To show $\blueD{6}+\blueD{6}+\blueD{6}+\blueD{6}$, we need to show $\maroonD{4}$ jumps of $\blueD{6}$ units.

![The same number line as above, with each arrow labeled, plus 6](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/012f7b3807b4611b15eaae06479fb6ecf85590a7)

$12+6=\purpleC{18}$

![The same number line with the first blank labeled 18](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fb4d0be0767691390f4a365826b655b94cb2d53d)

$18+6=\purpleC{24}$

![The same number line with the second blank labeled 24](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/31965da586be45c97ee260354e64a9ddcd2f16ff)

The last jump leaves us at $24$.

$\begin{align}
\maroonD{4} \times \blueD{6} &= \blueD{6}+\blueD{6}+\blueD{6}+\blueD{6}\\\\
&=24
\end{align}$

The completed number line:

![The completed number line with the first blank labeled 18 and the second blank labeled 24](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1ead7f6fbae2988023a4f2304f859ba0e29343ab)

$4\times 6=24$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the number line to evaluate $7\times 2$.**

[[☃ label-image 1]]

$7 \times 2 =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: $\maroonD{7} \times \blueD{2} = \underbrace{\blueD{2}+\blueD{2}+\blueD{2}+\blueD{2}+\blueD{2}+\blueD{2}+\blueD{2}}_{\maroonD{7}\blueD{\text{ twos}}}$

When we add a number, we jump that distance to the right on the number line.

To show $\blueD{2}+\blueD{2}+\blueD{2}+\blueD{2}+\blueD{2}+\blueD{2}+\blueD{2}$, we need to show $\maroonD{7}$ jumps of $\blueD{2}$ units.

![The same number line as above, with each arrow labeled, plus 2](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/859964cdd50e9d66d8ffef5a4e3377bb0f00549b)

$6+2=\purpleC{8}$

![The same number line with the first blank labeled 8](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4d37f15ec49f7375c0d6aeb8cf6618eedd416a2c)

$12+2=\purpleC{14}$

![The same number line with the second blank labeled 14](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/81b33a1eb1f872a4d30e18d2b7a3e90c07f9eb17)

The last jump leaves us at $14$.

$\begin{align}
\maroonD{7} \times \blueD{2} &= \blueD{2}+\blueD{2}+\blueD{2}+\blueD{2}+\blueD{2}+\blueD{2}+\blueD{2}\\\\
&=14
\end{align}$

The completed number line:

![The completed number line with the first blank labeled 8 and the second blank labeled 14](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ce3f411ec3d7d49f95093900bb143c91ecc23a63)

$7\times 2=14$
